pi_items.source: Code analysis: Verified in setup_task.sh lines 14, 19, 24, 32 using drupal_db_query and Drupal entity creation. Confirmed in export_result.sh line 26 in database queries. Matches Drupal taxonomy vocabulary machine name convention.

Code analysis: Verified in setup_task.sh line 57 (config name check), export_result.sh lines 12 and 14 (field.storage and field.field config names), export_result.sh line 33 (database table name taxonomy_term__field_category_image). Matches Drupal field naming convention (field_FIELDNAME).

Code analysis: Verified in setup_task.sh line 61 (config name check views.view.category_grid), export_result.sh lines 46, 53, 60 (config name and file path), export_result.sh line 68 (block plugin reference views_block:category_grid). Matches Drupal view machine name convention.

Code analysis: Verified in setup_task.sh line 23 where loop iterates over exactly these three terms. Each term is created via drush php:eval in lines 27-32. Confirmed in export_result.sh lines 25-27 where these exact term names are queried from the database.

Code analysis: Verified in setup_task.sh line 38 where IMAGE_DIR is set to this exact path. Used in lines 39 (mkdir), 40 (chown), 44-48 (image generation with convert), and 50 (chown again). This is a real directory path in the task container.

pi_items.verified_value: Synthetic - generated via ImageMagick 'convert' command during setup
pi_items.source: Code analysis: setup_task.sh lines 43-48 show images are generated using ImageMagick convert command: 'convert -size 600x600 xc:lightblue' (Headphones), 'xc:lightgreen' (Keyboards), 'xc:lightcoral' (Monitors). Images are not downloaded from external URLs but procedurally generated with text annotations.
Code analysis: setup_task.sh lines 44, 46, 48 all specify '-size 600x600' in the ImageMagick convert commands
Code analysis: setup_task.sh lines 44, 46, 48 all output to .jpg files (headphones.jpg, keyboards.jpg, monitors.jpg)

pi_items.verified_value: Image field attached to taxonomy_term entity type
pi_items.source: Code analysis: export_result.sh line 12 checks 'field.storage.taxonomy_term.field_category_image' (entity_type=taxonomy_term), line 14 checks field instance config, line 33 queries 'taxonomy_term__field_category_image' table (Drupal's dedicated field storage table for this field). Verifier.py line 26 confirms attachment to vocabulary.

pi_items.verified_value: Grid display showing terms with images
pi_items.source: Code analysis: export_result.sh line 60 checks for 'style_plugin: grid' in the view config YAML, line 61 checks 'base_table: taxonomy_term_field_data' to verify it displays terms, line 62 checks for 'field_category_image' field presence. Verifier.py lines 71-79 validate these properties.
Code analysis: export_result.sh line 77 checks for 'region: content' in the block configuration YAML. Verifier.py line 85-89 validates the block is in the content region.

privileged_info_summary: The task creates a visual category navigation system in Drupal Commerce using synthetic images. The 'Product Categories' vocabulary with machine name 'product_categories' contains three terms: Headphones, Keyboards, and Monitors. An Image field named 'Category Image' with machine name 'field_category_image' is added to this vocabulary. Three 600x600 JPEG images are generated (via ImageMagick, not external download) with these category names and stored in /home/ga/Downloads/category_images/. A View named 'Category Grid' with machine name 'category_grid' is created as a grid display of taxonomy terms with images, and placed as a block in the Content region of the homepage. All metadata claims about machine names, field configuration, and term names are accurate and match the Drupal configuration and database structure.
